summary: unifi-portal exfiltrates host identity to external endpoint on load
details: index.js imports `os` and `https` and, on module load, collects `os.hostname()`, `os.userInfo()`, and `os.networkInterfaces()` and transmits the data via `https.get` to an external destination referenced as hackerone.com. The version number (99.0.0) and package naming pattern are consistent with dependency-confusion research/PoC payloads targeting an internal `unifi-portal` namespace; in a victim build that resolves to the public registry, installing or requiring this package leaks host identity and network topology to a third party. Regardless of whether the recipient is a researcher or a malicious actor, the package's only behavior is unsolicited collection and exfiltration of installer host metadata.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
